transcript.clean_text: so on to the eighth and final stage of this 2019 parin 110 km to cover on a day littered with climes Egan Bernar with 45 seconds in hand as he looked to secure a sixth overall win in eight years for team sky but nid canana and the rest weren't going to give up without a fight Thomas Dent also hoping to wrap up victory in the mountains classification and surprise surprise he was part of the day's break away like yesterday a group of around 40 Riders getting away after the day's first climb Bob jungles Simon Yates and nner zacharin all in there a group of three trying to pull clear at the front with TJ vardan eventually looking to go It Alone kintana then attacks from the pelum with just under 50K to go M Star teammates Mark CER and Winan aona waiting for him a bit further up the road the Colombian going on to pick up three precious bonus seconds at the intermediate Sprint and By the 30k Mark he was the virtual Leader by just a handful of seconds Sky uping the tempo as they attempted to reel him in a game of cat and mouse on the approach to nice and with The Gap gradually dropping yon is a gear decided to try his luck from the leading group countering TJ van gardan and quickly pulling around 20 seconds clear the Spaniard crash with his brother Gawker on the descent into nice last year but no such trouble here his first stage success at Parry and that's already 18 wins this season for aana beral then rolling through a few seconds after kintana to claim the overall victory for Sky hand in hand with teammate Mikel kle kovy who had to settle for third place well it's been a Remar able performance from beral this week the 22-year-old showing incredible maturity as he mastered the Crosswinds the time trial and of course the climbs to finish 39 seconds clear of fellow Colombian kintana this his second win at a major stage race after last year's Tour of California he'll now be gearing up for a tilted a first Grand Tour Victory when he leads team sky at the jro Talia okay I think the thing that impresses me the most is the stuffed lion that you get for winning genuinely it's the lion I've always dreamed of getting my hands on it for as long as I can remember I know it's not the lion from the tour to France but it still means a great deal to me it's hugely important it's par and I honestly can't believe it so b a GE taking the stage eight win ahead of Oliver nasson and Wilco kelderman stage seven winner Danny Martinez for fourth Quintana in 10th only cloring back 7 seconds on beral slightly bigger gaps than last year overall when the top five finished within 20 seconds of each other but now 39 seconds clear of kintana K kovsky third and also taking the green jersey Jack heg and Roman B rounding out the top five thanks for joining me Andy Smith for this 77th edition of parin and do join us again next year
